spec_match.rationale: The page now models the current <usermedia> stream/error/cancel flow and setConstraints() setup rather than the old activate-event framing.

interactive.rationale: The flow simulator lets users compare immediate JavaScript, delayed JavaScript, native stream success, and native cancel/error paths.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The comparison now frames the trust and prompt-timing improvement of browser-owned <usermedia> activation accurately.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulator covers delayed callback risk, native success, cancellation, error, and unsupported fallback behavior.

references.rationale: The page includes the correct ChromeStatus reference and Media Capture links.

design_system.rationale: The simulator uses real buttons, stable panels, CSS variables, and no static-only code card.

summary: The static comparison has been upgraded into an interactive permission-flow simulator with current <usermedia> API names.
